column1: What's the difference between True/False/Not Given and Yes/No/Not Given?
column2:
True/False/Not Given = facts in the text. Yes/No/Not Given = opinions of the writer.
Cara membedakan: jika passage adalah teks faktual (sains, sejarah) gunakan TFNG; jika opinion piece (artikel argumentatif) gunakan YNNG.
Tips:
• "Not Given" = info tidak ada di teks
• "False/No" = info ada tapi BERTENTANGAN dengan teks
• Jangan pakai world knowledge — hanya teks!

column1: How to approach Matching Headings questions?
column2:
Read the heading list FIRST, then skim each paragraph for main idea (topic sentence + conclusion).
Strategi: jangan baca detail; cari ide utama tiap paragraf. Heading sering paraphrase, bukan kata persis.
Tips:
• Baca kalimat pertama & terakhir tiap paragraf
• Cross out heading yang sudah dipakai
• Sisakan yang paling sulit untuk terakhir
• Hati-hati dengan heading yang mirip (trap)

column1: How to approach Matching Paragraphs/Information questions?
column2:
Scan for specific info — questions are NOT in text order.
Strategi: baca pertanyaan dulu, garis bawahi keyword, lalu scan tiap paragraf. Jawaban bisa di paragraf manapun.
Tips:
• Keyword bisa di-paraphrase
• Satu paragraf bisa jadi jawaban beberapa soal
• Jangan urut — loncat-loncat
• Kerjakan yang mudah dulu

column1: How to approach Completion tasks (sentence/summary/note/table)?
column2:
Predict the word type (noun/verb/adjective) before scanning, then find the exact word in passage.
Strategi: baca konteks sekitar gap, prediksi grammar yang cocok, scan teks untuk kata persis.
Tips:
• Jawaban harus persis dari teks (tidak boleh diubah)
• Cek word limit (NO MORE THAN TWO/THREE WORDS)
• Cek grammar fit (singular/plural, tense)
• Summary completion sering di-paraphrase

column1: What is the multiple choice strategy in IELTS Reading?
column2:
Eliminate wrong options first — IELTS designs 2-3 plausible distractors per question.
Strategi: baca pertanyaan, prediksi jawaban, lalu cocokkan opsi. Jangan langsung pilih opsi yang "kelihatan benar".
Tips:
• Distractor sering pakai kata dari teks tapi makna beda
• "Partially true" = SALAH (harus FULLY true)
• Hati-hati extreme words: always, never, only
• Pertanyaan urut sesuai teks

column1: How to manage time in IELTS Reading (3 passages, 60 min)?
column2:
20 minutes per passage MAX. Passage 3 is hardest — don't run out of time.
Strategi: bawa jam tangan analog. Jangan terjebak di satu soal sulit lebih dari 1.5 menit.
Tips:
• Skip soal sulit, kembali nanti
• Transfer answer langsung di answer sheet (no extra time!)
• 5 menit terakhir: tebak semua yang kosong (no penalty)
• Latihan timer di rumah

column1: When to use skimming vs scanning in Reading?
column2:
Skim = read fast for general idea (matching headings). Scan = hunt for specific keyword (completion, T/F/NG).
Skimming: baca cepat (2-3 menit per passage) untuk tahu topik. Scanning: cari kata kunci spesifik tanpa baca semua.
Tips:
• Skim dulu sebelum lihat soal
• Scan untuk angka, nama, tanggal — mudah ditemukan
• Jangan baca kata per kata
• Latihan: skim artikel berita Bahasa Inggris

column1: How to handle unknown vocabulary in Reading?
column2:
Don't panic — use context clues, word parts, and skip if not needed.
Strategi: kata sulit sering tidak penting. Lihat kalimat sebelum/sesudah untuk konteks.
Tips:
• Cek prefix/suffix (un-, -tion, -able)
• Cek part of speech dari posisi
• Skip kalau tidak terkait dengan soal
• Jangan habiskan waktu translate semua

column1: What is the "Not Given" trap and how to avoid it?
column2:
If you can't find clear evidence in 30 seconds, the answer is likely "Not Given".
Trap: kandidat sering pilih True/False karena "merasa benar" — padahal info tidak disebutkan.
Tips:
• "Not Given" = teks DIAM tentang info ini
• Bedakan: tidak disebut (NG) vs bertentangan (F/No)
• Jangan asumsi dari logic atau pengetahuan umum
• Sekitar 30% jawaban biasanya NG

column1: What are the word limit rules (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS)?
column2:
Hyphenated words = 1 word. Numbers count as 1 word. Article (a/the) counts!
Aturan: "well-known" = 1 word, "21st" = 1 word, "the cat" = 2 words. Jika overlimit, otomatis SALAH.
Tips:
• Hitung kata sebelum menulis
• "NO MORE THAN TWO" = bisa 1 atau 2 kata
• Jangan tambah kata sendiri
• Spelling harus benar (typo = salah)

column1: Which Reading question types follow text order, which don't?
column2:
In order: T/F/NG, Y/N/NG, completion, multiple choice. NOT in order: matching headings, matching info, matching features.
Tips strategi: untuk soal urut, jawaban berikutnya pasti SETELAH jawaban sebelumnya di teks.
Tips:
• Pakai info ini untuk lokasi jawaban
• Matching = scan seluruh teks
• Hemat waktu dengan tahu pola ini
• Cek instruksi tiap section

column1: What is the IELTS Reading raw score to band conversion?
column2:
40/40 = 9.0; 35/40 = 8.0; 30/40 = 7.0; 27/40 = 6.5; 23/40 = 6.0; 19/40 = 5.5.
Target Nabil 6.5: butuh ~27 jawaban benar dari 40. Margin error sekitar 13 soal.
Tips:
• Academic Reading lebih ketat dari General
• Tidak ada minus untuk salah — JANGAN kosong!
• Tebak strategis (B/C paling sering benar di MC)
• Target 30/40 untuk safety margin 7.0

column1: How to approach Listening Section 1 (everyday conversation)?
column2:
Section 1 is easiest — mostly form filling (names, numbers, addresses). Listen for spelling and number traps.
Strategi: santai, tapi konsentrasi penuh karena traps di angka/spelling. Biasanya booking, registration, inquiry.
Tips:
• Speaker akan eja nama (catat huruf demi huruf)
• Hati-hati: "fifteen" vs "fifty"
• Format tanggal: 12th May atau May 12th
• Target 9-10/10 di section ini

column1: How to approach Listening Section 2 (monologue, social)?
column2:
One speaker giving info (tour, event, announcement). Often includes maps or diagrams.
Strategi: untuk map, ikuti arah speaker (turn left, opposite, next to). Pegang pensil di starting point.
Tips:
• Latih directions: north/south, opposite, between
• Map: speaker bergerak — ikuti petanya
• Multiple choice biasanya ada di sini
• Target 7-8/10

column1: How to approach Listening Section 3 (academic discussion)?
column2:
2-4 speakers discussing study/project. Watch for opinion changes and disagreements.
Strategi: identifikasi siapa berbicara. Speaker bisa berubah pikiran — jawaban = pendapat AKHIR.
Tips:
• "Actually, I think..." = ganti opinion
• "But on the other hand..." = trap
• MC sering tanya "what do they AGREE on?"
• Target 6-7/10 (lebih sulit)

column1: How to approach Listening Section 4 (academic lecture)?
column2:
One speaker, monologue, NO BREAK in middle. Hardest section — note/summary completion.
Strategi: scan semua 10 soal di awal (lebih banyak waktu preview). Akademik: science, history, business.
Tips:
• Tidak ada jeda — fokus penuh 5 menit
• Jawaban urut sesuai audio
• Vocabulary akademik (terminologi)
• Target 5-7/10

column1: How to handle unfamiliar accents in Listening?
column2:
IELTS uses British, Australian, American, Canadian, sometimes NZ. Train your ear to all accents.
Strategi: dengar BBC, ABC Australia, NPR sehari-hari. Jangan kaget jika beda dari TOEFL (US-only).
Tips:
• British: "schedule" = "shedule"
• Australian: vowels berubah ("today" = "todie")
• Listen to podcast 30 min/hari
• Subtitle off saat latihan

column1: What are common spelling mistakes in Listening?
column2:
Double letters (accommodation, necessary), silent letters (Wednesday), British vs American (colour vs color).
Spelling salah = jawaban salah meskipun dengar benar. British spelling preferred di IELTS.
Tips:
• Hafal: accommodation, occurred, necessary, beginning
• British: -our (colour), -re (centre), -ise (organise)
• Cek capital letters (proper nouns)
• Latihan dictation harian

column1: How to write numbers, dates, and times correctly?
column2:
Use digits for numbers (15, not fifteen). Dates: 12 May or May 12. Times: 7.30 pm or 19:30.
Format fleksibel tapi konsisten. Phone numbers: tulis tanpa spasi atau dash.
Tips:
• "Double 5" = 55
• "Oh" dalam phone = 0
• Year: 1990 = "nineteen ninety"
• Currency: $50 atau 50 dollars

column1: Why is reading questions BEFORE audio crucial?
column2:
You get 10-30 seconds preview before each section — USE IT to predict answers.
Strategi: di preview time, baca soal, garis bawahi keyword, prediksi tipe jawaban (number? name? noun?).
Tips:
• Underline keyword sebelum audio mulai
• Prediksi: angka? tempat? aktivitas?
• Jangan baca instruksi panjang — skip
• Saat audio, tangan siap menulis

column1: What is the "speaker correction" distractor trap?
column2:
Speaker says one answer, then corrects: "Actually, no, it's X." The LAST answer is correct.
Trap klasik: kandidat tulis jawaban pertama, padahal speaker mengoreksi diri.
Tips:
• Listen for: "actually", "sorry", "I mean", "let me check"
• "On second thought..." = correction coming
• Coret jawaban lama, tulis yang baru
• Siapkan pensil yang gampang dihapus

column1: What is the IELTS Listening raw score to band conversion?
column2:
40/40 = 9.0; 35/40 = 8.0; 30/40 = 7.0; 26/40 = 6.5; 23/40 = 6.0; 18/40 = 5.5.
Target Nabil 6.5: butuh ~26 jawaban benar dari 40. Listening sering jadi penyelamat skor overall.
Tips:
• Section 1 & 2 lebih mudah — target 8/10 each
• Section 3 & 4 — target 5-6/10
• Tidak ada minus — JANGAN kosong
• Latihan tiap hari (30 min)

column1: What is the Task 1 word count rule (Writing)?
column2:
Minimum 150 words. Under = penalty in Task Achievement (lose 1+ band).
Strategi: tulis 160-180 kata aman. Jangan over 200 (waste of time, penalty for irrelevance).
Tips:
• Hitung kata kasar: 10 kata per baris × 16 baris = 160
• Tulis full sentences, hindari list
• Examiner check word count
• Latihan estimasi visual

column1: What is the Task 2 word count rule (Writing)?
column2:
Minimum 250 words. Under = significant penalty (cap at 5.0 Task Response).
Strategi: tulis 270-300 kata. Lebih banyak ≠ lebih bagus jika quality menurun.
Tips:
• 4 paragraf × 65-75 kata = 270
• Conclusion harus complete (jangan dipotong)
• Hitung kasar tiap 50 kata
• Practice: tulis essay 270 dalam 40 min

column1: What is the structure of Task 1 (Academic)?
column2:
4 paragraphs: Introduction (paraphrase) + Overview (main trends) + Detail 1 + Detail 2.
Overview WAJIB ada — biasanya 2 kalimat utama tanpa angka. Detail = grouping data.
Tips:
• Intro: paraphrase question (don't copy)
• Overview: "Overall," + 2 main features
• Body: bandingkan/kelompokkan, sertakan angka
• Tidak ada conclusion separate

column1: What is the structure of Task 2 essay?
column2:
4 paragraphs: Introduction + Body 1 + Body 2 + Conclusion.
Setiap body = 1 main idea + explanation + example. Intro punya thesis statement (jawaban langsung).
Tips:
• Intro 40-50 kata: hook + paraphrase + thesis
• Body 80-100 kata: topic sentence + support
• Conclusion 40-50 kata: restate + final thought
• Total: 270-300 kata

column1: What are the Task 2 question types?
column2:
4 types: Opinion (agree/disagree), Discuss both views, Problem-Solution, Two-part question.
Identifikasi tipe SEBELUM menulis — struktur berbeda untuk tiap tipe.
Tips:
• Opinion: thesis jelas di intro & conclusion
• Discuss both: 1 paragraf each side, baru opinion
• Problem-Solution: 1 paragraf problem, 1 solution
• Two-part: jawab kedua pertanyaan eksplisit

column1: What is the time split for Writing (60 min)?
column2:
Task 1 = 20 min. Task 2 = 40 min. Do Task 2 FIRST if you struggle with time.
Strategi: Task 2 worth 2x more — prioritaskan. Sisakan 3-5 menit checking di akhir.
Tips:
• Task 2 dulu (40 min) — lebih penting
• Task 1 (20 min) — singkat & terstruktur
• 5 menit akhir: cek grammar/spelling
• Bawa jam tangan

column1: What are the 4 Writing marking criteria?
column2:
Task Achievement/Response, Coherence & Cohesion, Lexical Resource, Grammatical Range & Accuracy. Each 25%.
Examiner kasih band per kriteria, lalu rata-rata. Lemah di 1 kriteria = drop overall.
Tips:
• TA/TR: jawab semua bagian pertanyaan
• C&C: paragraf jelas + linking words tepat
• LR: variasi kosakata + collocations
• GRA: mix simple+complex, akurasi

column1: What is the most common Task Response failure?
column2:
Not addressing ALL parts of the question — partial answer = max 5.0.
Trap: pertanyaan punya 2 bagian, kandidat hanya jawab 1. Atau opinion tidak konsisten.
Tips:
• Underline kata kerja di question (discuss, give opinion)
• Cek: setiap bagian dijawab?
• Opinion harus jelas & konsisten
• Stick to topic — jangan melenceng

column1: What is the most common Coherence & Cohesion failure?
column2:
Overusing linking words (Firstly, Moreover, Furthermore in every sentence) = mechanical = lower band.
Trap: kandidat hafal list connector dan pakai semua. Examiner tahu ini kelemahan.
Tips:
• Variasi: gunakan referencing (this, these, such)
• 1-2 connector per paragraf cukup
• Pakai pronouns untuk cohesion
• Topic sentence > "Firstly"

column1: Why does Task 2 weigh more than Task 1?
column2:
Task 2 = 2/3 of Writing score. Task 1 = 1/3. Always prioritize Task 2 quality.
Implikasi: kalau Task 1 7.0 tapi Task 2 6.0, overall ≈ 6.5 (bukan 6.5 rata-rata).
Tips:
• Spend 40 min on Task 2 (no compromise)
• Task 2 weak = overall drop besar
• Latihan Task 2 lebih sering
• Master 4 question types Task 2

column1: What are the 4 Speaking marking criteria?
column2:
Fluency & Coherence, Lexical Resource, Grammatical Range & Accuracy, Pronunciation. Each 25%.
Examiner score real-time. Pronunciation BUKAN tentang accent — tentang clarity.
Tips:
• F&C: jangan banyak jeda/um/uh
• LR: variasi kata, idiom natural
• GRA: complex sentences kadang-kadang
• Pronunciation: word stress, intonation

column1: How to approach Speaking Part 1 (4-5 min)?
column2:
Familiar topics (home, work, hobbies). Answers should be 2-3 sentences — not too short, not too long.
Strategi: jawab + alasan + contoh kecil. Hindari "yes/no" jawaban pendek.
Tips:
• "Do you like coffee?" → "Yes, I drink it daily because..."
• Topics: family, study, hometown, food
• Relax — ini warm-up
• 3-4 questions per topic

column1: How to approach Speaking Part 2 (cue card)?
column2:
1 minute prep + 1-2 minutes talking. Cover ALL bullet points on card.
Strategi: di 1 menit prep, tulis 4-5 keyword (bukan kalimat). Talking: gunakan past tense + emotion.
Tips:
• Bullet points = struktur jawabanmu
• Tambah detail: when, where, who, why
• Akhiri dengan refleksi/perasaan
• Latihan dengan timer

column1: How to approach Speaking Part 3 (abstract discussion)?
column2:
Abstract questions related to Part 2 topic. EXTEND your answers (4-5 sentences).
Strategi: opini + alasan + contoh + counter-view. Tunjukkan critical thinking.
Tips:
• "In my opinion... because... for example..."
• "However, some might argue..."
• Topics: society, technology, education
• Jangan jawab pendek — kembangkan

column1: How to handle "I don't know" in Speaking?
column2:
NEVER say "I don't know" — speculate, give general answer, or talk about related topic.
Strategi: jika tidak tahu jawaban, ngomong tentang apa yang kamu tahu yang relevan.
Tips:
• "I'm not entirely sure, but I think..."
• "That's an interesting question. Perhaps..."
• "I haven't thought about it before, however..."
• Buy time: paraphrase pertanyaan dulu

column1: Pronunciation tips for Indonesian speakers?
column2:
Focus on: 'th' (think/this), p/b distinction, voiced consonants at word end, word stress.
Indonesian speakers sering masalah dengan: 'th' jadi 't', final consonant hilang, intonasi flat.
Tips:
• 'th': lidah di antara gigi (think, that)
• Final 'd', 'g', 'b' — JANGAN dihilangkan
• Word stress: PHOto vs phoTOgrapher
• Intonasi: naik-turun, jangan monoton

column1: Why shouldn't you memorize Speaking scripts?
column2:
Examiner is trained to detect memorized answers — automatic band drop. Sounds unnatural.
Trap: kandidat hafal jawaban Part 2 dari internet. Examiner langsung tahu (intonasi, kecepatan, vocabulary mismatch).
Tips:
• Hafal vocabulary & phrase, BUKAN kalimat utuh
• Latihan dengan topic baru tiap hari
• Natural pauses lebih baik dari hafalan lancar
• Be yourself — examiner mau tahu kamu

column1: How does body language affect Speaking score?
column2:
Confidence shows in voice — sit up straight, eye contact, smile. Affects pronunciation & fluency indirectly.
Bukan dinilai langsung, tapi mempengaruhi suara: posisi tubuh terbuka = suara lebih jelas & confident.
Tips:
• Duduk tegak, jangan menunduk
• Eye contact dengan examiner
• Senyum tipis — mengubah tone suara
• Tangan rileks (boleh gesture natural)
